codeReview.summary: The change correctly canonicalizes legacy anchor labels and adds regression coverage for empty anchor kinds and deleted-file lifecycle handling, but it introduces a blocking lifecycle regression: deleted-file reruns no longer fix previously matched findings once those findings already have last_seen_run_id set from an earlier rerun.

issues.description: `transitionMissingFinding` now returns early when `current.LastSeenRunID.Valid` before checking `reviewedPaths`, so a finding that persisted across one rerun and later disappears on a reviewed path is no longer transitioned to `fixed`. The feature changed deleted-file processing to populate `reviewedPaths` instead of calling `markDeletedPathMatches`, but without removing this early return, previously-seen findings on the deleted path remain active/stale instead of fixing. This breaks the requirement to preserve deleted-file fixes after canonicalization changes for findings that had already been carried forward via `last_seen_run_id`.

summary: Reviewed the feature handoff, transcript skeleton, commit 485400692bec75af63f23f4614d6865da8622ae6, and the relevant mission/shared-state files. The anchor canonicalization itself looks correct, but the persistence logic still fails a real deleted-file/fixed transition scenario once an existing finding already has `last_seen_run_id`, so this review fails with one blocking issue.
